w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: California K-12 Public School Projects Under DSA Jurisdiction
Wet-pipe automatic fire sprinkler systems for new construction and renovation projects at California public K-12 school facilities under the jurisdiction of the Division of the State Architect (DSA).
Aboveground wet-pipe sprinkler system piping, fittings, valves, and accessories.
Fire sprinkler heads for all hazard classifications.
Alarm valves and water flow detection devices.
System risers, control valve assemblies, and trim.
Seismic bracing and restraints for sprinkler piping per NFPA 13 (2025) Chapter 18 and ASCE 7-22.
Access panels for concealed valves and equipment.
Regulatory Framework - California K-12 DSA Projects:

Division of the State Architect (DSA): Primary authority having jurisdiction for plan review, approval, and construction oversight of fire sprinkler systems at California public K-12 schools per California Administrative Code (CAC), Title 24, Part 1.
California Building Code (CBC) 2025: Title 24, Part 2, Chapter 9 - Fire Protection and Life Safety Systems.
California Code of Regulations (CCR), Title 19, Division 1: State Fire Marshal requirements for inspection, testing, and maintenance.

California Education Code, Chapter 12.5 (commencing with Section 17070.10) (School Facility Program); and California Building Code (Title 24, Part 2), Section 903.2.19 and applicable subsections: Automatic sprinkler systems required throughout new state-funded
DSA Guideline GL-1: Project Submittal Guideline: Automatic Fire Sprinkler Systems.
DSA Interpretation of Regulations IR A-25: Design, Installation and Maintenance of Automatic Fire Sprinkler Systems.
DSA Policy PL 09-01: Fire Flow for Buildings.
DSA Policy PL 10-01: Plan Submittal Requirements: Automatic Fire Sprinkler Systems (AFSS).
DSA IR 16-13: Seismic Requirements for Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing Distribution Systems.

AHJ: Authority Having Jurisdiction. For California K-12 public schools, DSA serves as the primary AHJ during design, plan review, and construction phases. The Local Fire Authority (LFA) serves as AHJ for ongoing inspection and maintenance after project certification and occupancy.
DSA: Division of the State Architect. California state agency
design and construction oversight for K-12 schools, community colleges, and state essential services buildings.
LFA: Local Fire Authority. Local fire department or fire marshal with jurisdiction over ongoing fire safety inspections after building occupancy.
OSFM: Office of the State Fire Marshal (California).
Supplemental Sprinkler: Per NFPA 13 (2025) Section 3.3.223.3.6, a sprinkler installed below an obstruction greater than 4 feet wide.
Area of Discharge: Per NFPA 13 (2025) Section 3.3.8, the floor area covered by a sprinkler that
walls or obstructions whose summation determines the remote area.
IOR: Inspector of Record (DSA Project Inspector).
General: Complete wet-pipe automatic fire sprinkler system(s) complying with NFPA 13 (2025) and all applicable California codes for protection of K-12 school facilities.
System Type: Wet-pipe sprinkler system with piping containing water at all times. Water discharges immediately upon activation of individual sprinkler heads.
are indicated on Drawings for each area.
NFPA 13 (2025): Standard for the Installation of Sprinkler Systems.
NFPA 14 (2025): Standard for the Installation of Standpipe and Hose Systems.
NFPA 24 (2025): Standard for the Installation of Private Fire Service Mains and Their Appurtenances.
NFPA 25: Standard for the Inspection, Testing, and Maintenance of Water-Based Fire Protection Systems, California Edition, as adopted by CFC 2025.
NFPA 72 (2025): National Fire Alarm and Signaling Code.
ASCE/SEI 7-22: Minimum Design Loads and Associated Criteria for Buildings and Other Structures (seismic design requirements).
ASTM A53/A53M (latest edition): Standard Specification for Pipe, Steel, Black and Hot-Dipped, Zinc-Coated, Welded and Seamless.
ASTM A135/A135M (latest edition): Standard Specification for Electric-Resistance-Welded Steel Pipe.
ASTM A795/A795M (latest edition): Standard Specification for Black and Hot-Dipped Zinc-Coated (Galvanized) Welded and Seamless Steel Pipe for Fire Protection Use.
ANSI/AWWA C110: American Water Works Association - Ductile-Iron and Gray-Iron Fittings.
ANSI/AWWA C151/A21.51: Ductile-Iron Pipe, Centrifugally Cast.
ASME B16.3: Malleable Iron Threaded Fittings, Classes 150 and 300.
ASME B16.9: Factory-Made Wrought Buttwelding Fittings.
UL 199: Standard for Automatic Sprinklers for Fire-Protection Service.
UL 262: Standard for Gate Valves for Fire-Protection Service.
UL 312: Standard for Check Valves for Fire-Protection Service.
UL 789: Standard for Indicator Posts for Fire-Protection Service.
FM Approvals: Products listed in Approval Guide for fire protection equipment.
California Code of Regulations, Title 19, Division 1: State Fire Marshal.
California Health and Safety Code Sections 13195-13196.5.
Contract Documents include complete fire sprinkler system design prepared by the Fire Protection Engineer or Mechanical Designer of Record.

constitute complete fire sprinkler shop drawings. The Contractor shall install the system precisely as indicated on Drawings. Sprinkler head types, locations, orientations, deflector positions, pipe sizes, pipe routing, hanger locations, seismic brace locations, and all other system components are fully designed and detailed on the Drawings. The Contractor shall not modify, relocate, substitute, or deviate from any element shown on Drawings without a DSA-approved Construction Change Document (CCD).
Floor plans showing all sprinkler head locations with spacing dimensions.
Ceiling heights and types (flat, sloped, exposed structure).
all components, valve sizes, and elevation data.
Contract Documents have been submitted to DSA for review and approval.
Contractor shall not begin installation until DSA-approved drawings are available.
Contractor shall install system per DSA-approved Contract Documents.
C-16 Fire Protection Contractor license documentation.
OSFM fitter certifications for all installers per Cal Fire Information Bulletin 17-002.
Water Flow Test Data: If flow test data on Contract Documents is older than 12 months at time of construction, provide updated flow test "wet" signed by water purveyor or local fire authority.
Contractor shall verify all dimensions and conditions prior to installation.
Report any conflicts or discrepancies between Contract Documents and field conditions to Engineer immediately.
Do not deviate from Contract Documents without written authorization.
Any changes to approved sprinkler system design during construction shall be submitted to DSA via the CCD process
shall prepare CCD documents for DSA approval.
Field changes without DSA approval are not permitted.
Contractor shall notify Engineer of any field conditions requiring design changes.
Water supply data shown on Contract Documents is based on flow test conducted per DSA requirements.
Contractor shall verify water supply pressure and flow match design requirements prior to final connection.
If field-verified water supply differs from Contract Documents, notify Engineer immediately.
Per DSA BU 15-02: During declared drought conditions, coordinate with DSA for alternative flow test procedures.
Fire Sprinkler Contractor: California State Contractors License Board C-16 Fire Protection Contractor license.
Underground Contractor: California State Contractors License Board C-34 Pipeline Contractor license or C-16 license.
Fire Sprinkler Fitters: Certified by Cal Fire-Office of the State Fire Marshal per Information Bulletin 17-002. All
who plan, lay out, fabricate, install, alter, or repair water-based fire protection systems must be OSFM-certified.
shall have prior experience in similar
this project within the last 5 years.
All fire sprinkler system components shall be listed by a nationally recognized testing laboratory:
Other listing organizations acceptable to DSA.
Products shall be used only within the limitations of their listing.
Verify product listings match those specified in Contract Documents.
Conduct pre-installation meeting with Contractor, DSA Project Inspector, Engineer, and fire sprinkler subcontractor.
Review DSA-approved Contract Documents and specifications.
Coordinate work sequence with other trades.
Establish communication procedures for field issues and RFIs.
Contractor shall install system in strict accordance with DSA-approved Contract Documents.
shall use materials and products as specified or approved equal.
Workmanship shall comply with NFPA 13 (2025) requirements.
DSA Project Inspector shall observe installation and witness required tests.
Contractor shall not allow or cause any work to be covered up or concealed until it has been inspected, tested, and approved by DSA Project Inspector and all authorities having jurisdiction.

Should any work be covered up or concealed before such inspection, Contractor shall, at Contractor's own expense, uncover the work to the satisfaction of the DSA Project Inspector. All related costs shall be borne by the Contractor.
Submit product cut sheets for all components to Engineer for verification of compliance with Contract Documents:
Sprinkler heads (all types and temperature ratings).

Substitution Requests: If proposing products are different from those specified, submit substitution request with complete technical data demonstrating equivalence. Substitutions require Engineer approval and may require DSA CCD.

submittals are for verification only and do not constitute approval to deviate from specified products. Where submitted products differ from those specified, a substitution request per Paragraph 1.07.A.2 is required
Fitter Certifications: OSFM certification documentation for all installers.
Manufacturer Certifications: Written statements from manufacturers certifying products are suitable for intended application.
Updated Water Flow Test: If flow test on Contract Documents is older than 12 months, provide current test data "wet" signed by water purveyor or LFA.
As-Built Drawings: Provide record drawings marked up to show actual installed conditions including:
Any field-approved deviations from Contract Documents.
Actual sprinkler head locations if adjusted for field conditions.
Functional test reports (alarms, drains, tamper switches).
Contractor's Material and Test Certificate: Per NFPA 13 (2025) Chapter 29 and CCR Title 19 requirements.
Testing and maintenance schedules per NFPA 25 California Edition.
Spare Parts: Provide spare sprinkler cabinet with:
Minimum 6 spare sprinklers of each type/temperature rating installed, or quantity per NFPA 13 Table 6.2.9, whichever is greater.
Mounted in readily accessible location per NFPA 13 (2025) Section 6.2.9.
List of sprinklers in cabinet posted inside cabinet door.
Warranty Documentation: Written warranty from Contractor.
Deliver materials in original, unopened containers with manufacturer's labels intact.
Verify all materials match approved submittals.
Inspect materials upon delivery for damage.
Store materials in clean, dry, protected location.
Protect sprinkler heads from physical damage.
Store pipe elevated off ground on suitable supports.
Protect threaded ends and grooves from damage.
Handle materials carefully to prevent damage.
Do not drop or throw sprinkler heads.
Protect glass bulbs and fusible elements from damage.
Sprinklers that have been dropped, damaged, have cracked bulbs, or show visible loss of fluid shall not be installed.
Wet-pipe systems shall only be installed in areas where ambient temperatures remain above 40 degrees F (4 degrees C).
Verify field dimensions and conditions prior to fabrication.
Coordinate ceiling heights, structural conditions, and obstructions.
Coordinate with other trades for available space.

Seismic Design Category as determined by project-specific geotechnical data and ASCE 7-22 for the applicable Risk Category, but no less than SDC D per CBC Section 1613A.2.5. Refer to project structural documents for site-specific seismic design parameters.
Seismic bracing is required for all fire sprinkler systems in California K-12 schools.
Component Importance Factor (Ip): 1.5 for fire sprinkler systems per ASCE 7-22.
Finish color requirements for exposed sprinklers and piping.
Routing of pipe in exposed areas subject to Architect’s approval.
Water supply connection and backflow prevention.

Contractor shall cooperate and coordinate work with all other trades. Piping runs found to be in conflict with the work of other trades, as a result of neglected coordination, shall be removed and reinstalled in new locations designated by the Architect at no additional cost to the Owner.
Connect waterflow switches to building fire alarm system.
Connect tamper switches on all control valves to fire alarm system.
Coordinate supervision requirements with fire alarm contractor.
Provide written warranty covering materials and workmanship for minimum 1 year from date of DSA project certification.
Warranty shall cover repair or replacement of defective materials and correction of defective workmanship at no additional cost to Owner.
If during the warranty period any material, equipment, or any part of the system fails to function properly, Contractor shall make good the defects without any expense to the Owner.
Sprinkler heads: Provide manufacturer's standard limited lifetime warranty.
Basis of Design: The following manufacturers are listed as basis of design. Other manufacturers producing equivalent products listed by UL or FM may be submitted for approval:
: Stainless steel (Type 304), vandal-proof Allen wrench lock – for locations requiring stainless steel per Article 2.11.
Elmdor (Model DW-AKL): Galvannealed steel with Allen key latch – for drywall applications where stainless steel is not required.
In-Building Fire Service Riser (from underground entry to aboveground system):
Ames (Watts Water Technologies) or approved equal.
UL listed and FM approved for fire protection service.
Size: As indicated on DSA-approved drawings.
All sprinkler heads shall be listed by UL or FM for their intended application.
All sprinkler heads shall be new. Relocation of existing sprinklers is not permitted.
Sprinkler heads shall be compatible with pipe connection type.
Sprinklers shall be glass bulb type.
Sprinklers shall be same manufacturer throughout the project.
Sprinkler head type, rating, and response are scheduled on the drawings.
Provide with matching cover plate in ceiling color.
Cover plate release temperature: Shall be rated to release at a temperature lower than the sprinkler activation temperature, in accordance with the manufacturer's listing.
Factory installed escutcheon and cover plate.
Standard brass, chrome, or white factory finish as scheduled.
Custom colors: Factory-applied finish. Field painting of sprinkler heads is not permitted.
Exposed piping in finished spaces: Match sprinkler finish.
Required sprinkler guards are shown on DSA approved drawings.
Sprinkler guards shall utilize a bolt-on feature to the base of the sprinkler or be a factory-installed guard. Wire cage type, listed for fire sprinkler service.
Retard feature: Adjustable, factory set at 30 seconds. Maximum retard setting shall not exceed 90 seconds.
Contact rating: Suitable for fire alarm panel.
Minimum 1-inch valve with orifice equivalent to smallest sprinkler in system.
All control valves shall be listed indicating-type valves.
All shutoff valves including PIV and backflow preventer shall be electronically supervised.
OS&Y Gate Valves (Outside Screw and Yoke):
UL listed, 175 PSI minimum working pressure.
Wall post indicator valve (WPIV) or standard PIV as indicated.
UL listed indicating butterfly valve for fire protection service.
Grooved-end type for grooved piping systems.
Visual indicator for open/closed position, easily readable from floor level.
Minimum 300 PSI rated working pressure for sprinkler
Bubble-tight shutoff at rated working pressure.
DPDT supervisory (monitor) switch, compatible with building fire alarm system.
UL listed for fire protection service.
Use for 2-inch and smaller drain and test connections.
UL/FM listed for fire protection service.
Bronze, 2 inches and smaller, screwed-in bonnet, threaded ends, rising stem.
Swing check type for horizontal installation.
Grooved end or flanged end as required.
Pressure Relief Valves: Provide listed pressure relief valves in accordance with the currently adopted NFPA 13 requirements for wet-system overpressure protection.
Setpoint: 175 psig or 10 psig above the maximum normal static system pressure (downstream of any backflow/PRV that defines the zone).
Provide fire department connection(s) as indicated on Drawings.
Location: Visible and accessible from fire apparatus access road.
Per CBC Section 912: Coordinate thread type with local fire department.
female swivel inlets with threads matching local fire department (typically 2-1/
Brass body with chrome or satin finish.
Clapper-type check valves in each inlet.
Plugs or breakable caps with chains.
UL listed freestanding or post-type connection.
Height: 36 to 42 inches to center of inlets.
Concrete pad base or bollard protection as indicated.
Provide listed check valve in FDC supply pipe.
Install between FDC and system riser.
per NFPA 13 and local fire department requirements.
Sign shall indicate "AUTO SPKR" or "AUTOMATIC SPRINKLER".
Comply with CBC Section 912.4 for FDC signage.
per ASTM A53 or ASTM A795:
per ASTM A135 or ASTM A795:
2 inches and larger: Grooved mechanical joints only.
Cast iron or malleable iron conforming to ASME B16.3 (malleable iron) or ASME B16.4 (gray iron).
Listed rigid and flexible couplings per application.
Rigid couplings: Use for standard runs and where rigidity is required.
Flexible couplings: Use at equipment connections, building expansion joints, and seismic separation joints.
Gaskets: EPDM rated for potable water service. Gaskets shall be molded and produced by the coupling manufacturer.
Housing: Ductile iron per manufacturer's standard.
Grooved ends shall be clean and free from indentations, projections, and roll marks in the area from end to groove.
Mechanical-tee branch outlet fittings shall be UL Listed and FM Approved for fire sprinkler service.
may be used to form branch outlets from steel pipe mains and branch lines in wet-pipe sprinkler systems
permitted by NFPA 13 and the fitting listing.
shall be installed only on pipe sizes and wall thicknesses permitted by the fitting manufacturer.
Outlets shall be threaded or grooved as required to match the connected piping.
Mechanical-tee outlets shall not be used where prohibited by sprinkler manufacturer listings, including installations for dry sprinklers unless specifically listed for that purpose.
Brass nipples at sprinkler drops in armover configurations.
150 PSI malleable iron, black or galvanized to match pipe.
Close nipples and threaded unions are prohibited. Where a threaded union or coupling is needed, a groove type fitting shall be used.
One-piece reducing fittings shall be used wherever a change is made in pipe size.
and reducing couplings shall not be permitted.
Cast iron, class 125, conforming to ASME B16.1.
All hangers shall be listed for fire sprinkler service.
Maximum hanger spacing per DSA approved drawings.
Hanging, bracing, and supports shall be by one manufacturer and listed as an approved assembly.
Trapeze hangers: For multiple parallel runs.
Minimum 3/8-inch diameter threaded rod for pipe 4-inch and smaller.
Minimum 1/2-inch diameter threaded rod for pipe size 5-inch.
Minimum rod sizes for pipe 6-inch and larger per NFPA 13 Table 17.2.1.1 (hanger rod sizes) and Table 17.2.
Seismic bracing shall comply with NFPA 13 (2025) Chapter 18, ASCE 7-22 and DSA approved drawings.
Lateral Braces: Resist horizontal seismic forces perpendicular to pipe.
Longitudinal Braces: Resist horizontal seismic forces parallel to pipe.
4-Way Braces: Resist horizontal seismic forces in all directions.
Per DSA approved drawings and fire protection product data submitted to DSA by design engineer.
Per DSA approved drawings and fire protection product data submitted to DSA by design engineer.
Seismic separation assembly shall be UL/FM listed swing joint assembly rated at a minimum of 175 PSI.
Per DSA approved drawings and fire protection product data submitted to DSA by design engineer.
Provide durable identification for sprinkler piping, control valves, test/drain assemblies, fire department connections, and riser room(s).
Materials: engraved rigid plastic or metal signs; corrosion-resistant wire/chain or mechanical fasteners; indoor/outdoor rated.
Provide a permanent sign at each sprinkler system control valve indicating:
Minimum 1/2-inch white letters on red background, or per NFPA 170 and local fire authority requirements.
Hydraulic data nameplate at each riser showing:
Contractor's name and C-16 license number.
"AUTOMATIC SPRINKLER" or "AUTO SPKR" sign per CBC Section 912.4.
Indicating signs directing fire department to
Red lettering on white background: "FIRE SPRINKLER" with directional arrows.
Provide pipe labels on sprinkler mains, risers, and drains.
Provide access panels at all concealed valves, drain connections, and equipment requiring periodic access for inspection or maintenance.
construction. Stainless steel (Type 304) at locations indicated on Drawings or where required by environmental conditions. Galvannealed steel
at interior drywall locations where stainless steel is not required.
Vandal-proof door lock operated by Allen wrench.
Size: As required to provide adequate access to concealed components. Minimum 12 inches by 12 inches.
Pipe through ceilings at sprinkler head locations shall be furnished with a two-piece or fully concealed escutcheon.
Escutcheons shall be identical and match throughout the building for each application type.
Escutcheons in areas with finished ceilings: White, or as selected by Architect.
Automatic air venting valve for wet-pipe sprinkler systems.
or PDC Series vibrating type bells as required by system design and power source at bell location.
Outdoor use requires weatherproof back box.
Dial: White aluminum with stop pin; black and red lettering.
Compatible with building fire alarm system.
Two-switch design (one for supervisory, one for trouble) where required by fire alarm system.
all rough-in conditions are ready for sprinkler installation.
Verify structural framing is complete and accessible.
Examine areas and conditions under which work shall be performed. Correct conditions
to proper and timely completion of work. Do not proceed until unsatisfactory conditions have been corrected.
Verify HVAC ductwork installation is coordinated.
Verify electrical conduit routing is coordinated.
Verify underground piping is complete and tested.
Verify water supply pressure and flow match design requirements.
Conduct final water flow test prior to system completion.
Protect installed work from damage by other trades.
Cap open pipe ends at end of each workday.
Protect sprinkler heads from paint, debris, and physical damage.
Sprinkler bulb protectors shall be removed by hand only after installation is complete and area is clean.
Contractor shall be held responsible for all damage to equipment and material until written notice of acceptance from the Engineer.
Remove all cutting oil, debris, and foreign material.
Remove scale and foreign matter from inside and outside of pipes before assembly.
Prepare grooved ends per coupling manufacturer's requirements.
Clean and prepare threads for proper engagement.
Provide auxiliary drains at all trapped piping per NFPA 13.
Do not penetrate structural members without structural engineer approval.
All drain valves shall be plugged and piped to accessible location. Those installed within walls shall be equipped with locking access panels.
Apply pipe joint compound or Teflon tape to male threads only.
Engage minimum number of threads per NFPA 13.
Do not back off fittings after threading.
Cut grooves per coupling manufacturer's requirements.
Verify groove dimensions with go/no-go gauge.
Tighten bolts evenly to proper torque.
Grooved joints shall be installed in accordance with the manufacturer's latest published installation instructions.
Install mechanical tees in accordance with the fitting manufacturer's installation instructions including:
Proper hole cutting or drilling procedures.
Correct gasket placement and bolt torque.
Inspect mechanical-tee installations for proper hole size, gasket seating, and bolt tightening.
Replace fittings installed on improperly prepared pipe.
Welding shall be performed by certified welders.
Follow welding procedures per NFPA 13 (2025) Section 10.4 and applicable AWS standards.
Clean interior of pipe after welding.
Maximum deflection of branch lines: Per sprinkler listing.
Support branch lines within 3 feet of sprinkler head.
Armover unsupported length: Per NFPA 13 (2025) requirements for applicable pipe size.
Provide base support for riser weight.
Install flexible coupling within 24 inches of top of riser.
Install 1/2-inch pressure relief valve at riser.
Sprinkler system shall be provided with complete drainage facilities in accordance with NFPA 13 and CBC standards.
System main drain shall be piped to nearest standpipe drain in riser room with air gap.
Install sprinkler heads only after rough-in piping is complete and flushed.
Use only manufacturer's recommended wrench for installation.
Do not grip or damage deflector, frame, or heat-responsive element.
Apply Teflon tape to threads per manufacturer's instructions.
Install sprinkler heads at locations, types, spacing, and deflector positions shown on DSA-approved drawings.
Position sprinkler deflectors parallel to ceiling.
Maintain clearances from obstructions per NFPA 13 (2025) Chapter 9 and DSA-approved drawings.
g, coverage areas, and wall distances are indicated on Drawings and comply with NFPA 13.
Verify proper fit and adjustment of escutcheon/cover plate.
Verify cover plate is properly seated and aligned.
Protect sprinkler heads with factory-provided caps until area is clean.
Replace any sprinkler that has been painted, damaged, or loaded.
Do not hang materials or equipment from sprinkler piping.
Install seismic braces per approved DSA drawings.
Maximum angles per NFPA 13 (2025): 45 degrees from horizontal.
Minimum angles per NFPA 13 (2025): 30 degrees from horizontal for steel pipe.
First lateral brace within 6 feet of riser.
Maximum spacing per DSA approved drawings.
Brace to structure on opposite side of pipe from lateral load direction.
Maximum spacing per DSA approved drawings.
Restrain branch lines at locations shown on DSA approved drawings.
Install flexible couplings within 24 inches of top of riser.
At system riser after flange connection to AMES in-building-riser and before check valve.
Install control valves in accessible locations.
Provide minimum 18-inch clearance in front of valves.
Install tamper switches on all control valves.
Height: Inlets 18 inches minimum, 42 inches maximum above grade.
Install check valve in FDC supply line.
Provide signage per NFPA 13 and CBC requirements.
Install at hydraulically most remote point.
Pipe discharge to exterior or visible drain location.
Connect to fire alarm system per Division 28 requirements.
Set retard time per local fire authority requirements (typically 30-90 seconds).
Locate relief valve in riser room in a visible and accessible location.
Provide clearance for inspection, service, and replacement without removing other equipment.
Pipe relief valve discharge full size to a safe, observable location where discharge will not cause injury or property damage.
Terminate discharge at an approved drain receptor with an air gap.
Provide a written test plan describing how hydrostatic testing will be performed without leaving the system unprotected at turnover.
Where lockable relief valves are used for hydrostatic testing, lock/return valves to normal operating condition after testing and provide tamper-evident seal.
valve listing, size, nominal setting, discharge routing, and absence of leakage at normal system pressure.
Do not connect aboveground sprinkler system piping to the underground fire service lead-in until:
Underground flushing and hydrostatic testing are completed and accepted
The Contractor’s Material and Test Certificate for Underground Piping has been submitted.
Underground Flushing Requirements per NFPA 13 (2025) Section 28.1 and NFPA 24:
Flush all underground piping from water supply to system riser before
Minimum flushing flow rates to achieve 10 feet per second velocity:
Use burlap bags or strainers at flush outlet to capture debris.
Continue flushing until discharge water is clear and free of debris.
Underground flushing shall be witnessed by DSA Project Inspector.
Aboveground Flushing Requirements per NFPA 13 (2025) Section 28.2:
Flush all aboveground piping before installing sprinkler heads.
Branch lines may be flushed through end-of-line test connections or open fittings.
Do not install sprinkler heads until flushing is complete.
Provide flushing connections at end of each cross main.
Minimum flush connection size per pipe being flushed:
2-inch and smaller pipe: Minimum 2-inch flush connection.
4-inch and larger pipe: Minimum 4-inch flush connection.
Properly restrain and secure all flushing piping and hoses.
Document flushing operation including date, duration, and observations.
Note clarity of water and any debris removed.
Provide signed flushing certificate to DSA Project Inspector.
Flushing certification is required before system acceptance.
Hydrostatic Test Requirements per NFPA 13 (2025) Chapter 29:
Test all new piping and attached appurtenances after installation and before concealment.
High Pressure Systems: Where system working pressure exceeds 150 PSI, test at 50 PSI above maximum system working pressure for 2 hours per Section 29.2.1.3.
Acceptance Criteria per Section 29.2.1.2: No loss in gauge pressure or visual leakage.
Include Fire Department Connection (FDC) piping in hydrostatic test.
Install sprinkler heads after piping passes hydrostatic
use approved test plugs per Section 29.2.1.9.
Fill system slowly from lowest point to allow air to escape through high point vents.
Apply test pressure using calibrated hydrostatic test pump.
Maintain test pressure for minimum 2 hours.
Visually inspect all joints, fittings, and connections for leakage during test.
Record pressure gauge readings at start and end of test.
Confirm pressure at highest elevation meets requirements (elevation loss is acceptable
Coordinate with DSA Project Inspector for test witnessing.
Provide minimum 48-hour advance notice before testing.
All hydrostatic tests shall be witnessed by DSA Project Inspector.
Have approved drawings available at time of test.
Record test pressure, duration, and gauge readings.
Document pass/fail and any repairs made.
Obtain DSA Project Inspector signature on test certificate.
Certify that no additives, corrosive chemicals, sodium silicate, brine, or other corrosive substances were used for testing or stopping leaks.
Main Drain Test per NFPA 13 (2025) Section 29.2.6:
Conduct main drain test with DSA Project Inspector present.
Record static pressure with main drain valve closed.
Record residual pressure with drain fully open.
shall be piped to exterior or visible interior
Document static and residual pressures on Contractor's Material and Test Certificate.
Results establish baseline for future annual main drain tests per NFPA 25.
Waterflow Alarm Test per NFPA 13 (2025) Section 29.2.5:
Open inspector's test connection at hydraulically remote location.
Verify waterflow switch activates and transmits signal to fire alarm panel.
Record time from test valve opening to alarm activation (shall not exceed 90 seconds).
Verify correct identification of each zone/device at fire alarm control panel.
Close test connection and verify alarm restores.
Tamper Switch Test per NFPA 13 (2025) Section 29.2.4:
Verify all control valves are in normal (open) position.
Close each control valve partially (two turns maximum from fully open).
Verify tamper switch activates supervisory signal at fire alarm panel.
Return valve to fully open position.
Verify supervisory signal restores at fire alarm panel.
Backflow Preventer Forward Flow Test per NFPA 13 (2025) Section 29.2.9:
Where backflow preventer is installed in sprinkler supply, conduct forward flow test.
Flow water through system at system demand rate (or maximum achievable flow).
Verify backflow preventer check valves operate correctly.
Fire Department Connection Test per NFPA 13 (2025) Section 29.2.3:
Verify FDC clapper valves operate freely.
Verify FDC check valve holds under system pressure.
Verify FDC piping was included in hydrostatic test.
Verify threads are compatible with local fire department.
Verify signage is installed per NFPA 13 and CBC.
Pressure Gauge Calibration per NFPA 13 (2025) Section 29.2.7:
Verify all pressure gauges are installed per approved drawings.
Gauges shall be listed and suitable for fire protection service.
Calibrate or replace gauges not accurate within 3% of full scale.
Sign and Nameplate Verification per NFPA 13 (2025) Section 29.2.8:
Verify hydraulic design nameplate is installed at system riser.
Verify all required signage is installed:
Per DSA requirements, notify local fire authority of scheduled testing.
Invite local fire authority to witness tests (at school district's discretion).
Provide test results to local fire authority after acceptance.
Any deficiencies identified during testing shall be corrected and retested at no additional cost to the Owner until full compliance is achieved.
Provide Contractor's Material and Test Certificate per NFPA 13 (2025) Chapter 29 including:
Contractor's name, address, and C-16 license number.
Name of property owner and property address.
Certification that installation complies with NFPA 13 and approved plans.
Provide minimum 2-hour training session for Owner's maintenance personnel.
Location of control valves and components.
Inspection, testing, and maintenance requirements per NFPA 25 California Edition.
Spare sprinkler head cabinet location and use.
When to contact fire sprinkler service contractor.
Recommended maintenance schedule per NFPA 25 California Edition.
Manufacturer's literature for all installed components.
As-built drawings showing any deviations from Contract Documents and location of all system drawings.
Coordinate final inspection with DSA Project Inspector.
All punch list items shall be complete before final inspection.
Provide all required documentation for DSA project certification.
Final acceptance of the fire sprinkler system shall be subject to approval by DSA, IOR, and AHJ. System shall not be placed in service until all testing and documentation have been approved.
Signed Contractor's Material and Test Certificate.
Provide copies of all test reports to local fire authority.
Fire sprinkler system must be complete and operational before occupancy per CBC.
Ongoing inspection, testing, and maintenance becomes responsibility of Owner and local fire authority after DSA project certification.
Remove rubbish, debris, and waste material and legally dispose
